summary: Install-time env-var and filesystem exfiltration to hardcoded remote IP
details: On npm install, postinstall.js performs unconditional exfiltration of installer-side data to a hardcoded bare-IP endpoint http://154.57.164.70:30569/api/modules/ECT-472839. The script iterates all process.env entries (postinstall.js:55), reads candidate secret/flag files from system paths (/, /root, /app, /tmp, /home), and shells out via execFileSync('/bin/sh', '-c',...) to run `find` and `grep -RaoE 'HTB\{[^}]+\}'` across /home, /app, /opt, /srv, /tmp, /workspace, /usr/src/app, and the current working directory (postinstall.js:80). Harvested data is PUT as JSON to the attacker endpoint (postinstall.js:117). Additionally, the script writes attacker-controlled content into web server document roots (/var/www/html/flag.txt, /usr/share/nginx/html/flag.txt, etc.), creating directories as needed (postinstall.js:137), enabling follow-on defacement or served-payload exploitation. Although the package self-describes as a CTF/supply-chain test payload, the code is a fully functional exfiltration and host-tampering primitive that harms any installer.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
